Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
My Review: A perfect novel. Every sentence earns its place.

Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
Author: Harper Lee
My Review: Timeless and devastating.

Author: George Orwell
My Review: More relevant than ever.

Title: Pride and Prejudice
Author: Jane Austen
My Review: Witty and surprisingly modern.

Title: The Catcher in the Rye
Author: J.D. Salinger
My Review: I get why people love it but it wasn't for me.

Title: Beloved
Author: Toni Morrison
My Review: Haunting. The prose is like nothing else.

Title: The Road
Author: Cormac McCarthy
My Review: Bleak but beautiful. Read in two sittings.

Title: A Gentleman in Moscow
Author: Amor Towles
My Review: Charming, witty, and deeply humane. One of my favorites this year.

Title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
Author: Yuval Noah Harari
My Review: Changed how I think about history.

Title: Educated
Author: Tara Westover
My Review: Incredible memoir. Couldn't put it down.

Title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
Author: Rebecca Skloot
My Review: Equal parts science and human story.

Title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
Author: Daniel Kahneman
My Review: Dense but rewarding. Took me a while to finish.

Title: Born a Crime
Author: Trevor Noah
My Review: Funny and heartbreaking. The audiobook is even better.

Title: Atomic Habits
Author: James Clear
My Review: Practical and actionable.

Title: The Body: A Guide for Occupants
Author: Bill Bryson
My Review: Fascinating as always from Bryson.

Title: Quiet: The Power of Introverts in a World That Can't Stop Talking
Author: Susan Cain
My Review: Validating read for any introvert.

Title: Range: Why Generalists Triumph in a Specialized World
Author: David Epstein
My Review: Great counterpoint to the 10000 hours idea.

Title: Say Nothing: A True Story of Murder and Memory in Northern Ireland
Author: Patrick Radden Keefe

Title: Dune
Author: Frank Herbert
My Review: Epic worldbuilding, deeply philosophical.

Title: The Left Hand of Darkness
Author: Ursula K. Le Guin
My Review: Ahead of its time in every way.

Title: Neuromancer
Author: William Gibson
My Review: Influential but dated prose. Glad I read it.

Title: Project Hail Mary
Author: Andy Weir
My Review: Best sci-fi I've read in years. Funny, smart, emotional.

Title: The Fifth Season
Author: N.K. Jemisin
My Review: Second-person POV was jarring at first but brilliant.

Title: Piranesi
Author: Susanna Clarke
My Review: Dreamlike and mysterious. Very short but stays with you.

Title: Deep Work
Author: Cal Newport
My Review: Applied the time-blocking method immediately.

Title: The Psychology of Money
Author: Morgan Housel
My Review: Short chapters that each land a punch.

Title: Digital Minimalism
Author: Cal Newport
My Review: Good ideas but a bit repetitive.

Title: Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals
Author: Oliver Burkeman

Title: Essentialism: The Disciplined Pursuit of Less
Author: Greg McKeown
My Review: Covers similar ground to Deep Work.

Title: The Pragmatic Programmer
Author: David Thomas
My Review: Every developer should read this. The 20th anniversary edition is excellent.

Title: Clean Code
Author: Robert C. Martin
My Review: Some good principles but very Java-centric. Dated in parts.

Title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
Author: Martin Kleppmann
My Review: The best technical book I've ever read. Dense, thorough, brilliant.

Title: A Philosophy of Software Design
Author: John Ousterhout
My Review: Short and opinionated. Pairs well with Clean Code.

Title: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code
Author: Martin Fowler

Title: The Mythical Man-Month
Author: Frederick P. Brooks Jr.
My Review: Still relevant after 50 years. That says something.
